acceptance_targets: [CH15_CONTINUITY_CROOK_ROSTER] By the end of the revised opening and casualty report, a cold reader can account for every sin-eater established at the Crook in the boundary context and every named casualty or survivor in this chapter without guessing. No previously established Crook member disappears, and no new death or survivor appears, without an explicit on-page explanation anchored in the revised lines corresponding to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:7-11 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:215.

[CH15_PRESPENDS_BOWL_PAYOFF] In the revised Chapter 15, the bowl may be carried, set down, or damaged, but the prose does not describe it as already doing unmistakably real sin-eating work or completing its fake-to-real symbolic turn. A cold reader moving from the revised Chapter 15 into Chapter 16 should still be able to experience the Chapter 16 bowl beat as the first clear moment the object performs genuine ritual work.

[CH15_AWARD_OVEREXPLAINS_CAUL_BREAK] After the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:183-203, every subsequent paragraph in the escape/ridge section visibly does one of four things: changes the escape situation, reveals a new survival fact, forces a new choice, or alters a relationship stance. If a later paragraph only rephrases Caul's broken faith or the System's betrayal without adding one of those concrete functions, the chapter still fails this test.
[R-10] Ch 15 em-dash density is at or below 15% of prose lines.

findings.acceptance_test: By the end of the revised opening and casualty report, a cold reader can account for every sin-eater established at the Crook in the boundary context and every named casualty or survivor in this chapter without guessing. No previously established Crook member disappears, and no new death or survivor appears, without an explicit on-page explanation anchored in the revised lines corresponding to snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:7-11 and snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:215.
findings.problem: The Crook roster does not reconcile with the boundary context from the end of Chapter 14. This chapter silently swaps the established camp cast for Errin, Paran, Goss, Kael, and Renna, then builds its casualty math around that new set. Because the deaths and escape stakes depend on who was actually present at the Crook, the unexplained roster replacement reads as a visible continuity contradiction, not harmless name churn.
findings.rewrite_direction: Revise the camp-establishing material at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:7; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:9; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:11 and the evacuation/casualty accounting at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:51; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:99; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:215 so the Crook roster matches prior establishment. Either rename the Chapter 15 sin-eaters to the already-established Crook cast, or add explicit on-page reconciliation in the opening that explains overnight arrivals/departures before the attack begins. Keep the dead/survived count internally consistent once arrows start landing.

findings.acceptance_test: In the revised Chapter 15, the bowl may be carried, set down, or damaged, but the prose does not describe it as already doing unmistakably real sin-eating work or completing its fake-to-real symbolic turn. A cold reader moving from the revised Chapter 15 into Chapter 16 should still be able to experience the Chapter 16 bowl beat as the first clear moment the object performs genuine ritual work.
findings.problem: The narration already converts Maren's ceramic bowl from fake prop into a 'beginner's tool' of real practice and gives it a climactic crack during the nexus consumption. The context pack tracks the bowl's fake-to-real turn as a Chapter 16 payoff, so Chapter 15 partially spends that object beat a chapter early and weakens the next chapter's intended first-real-use force.
findings.rewrite_direction: Adjust the bowl handling at snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:5; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:19; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:123; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:139; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:189 so the bowl can remain physically present without being framed as the already-transformed instrument of genuine sin-eating. Let the nexus consumption turn on Maren's body, the opening, and the contamination itself; reserve the explicit fake-prop-becomes-real thematic conversion for Chapter 16's separation ritual.

findings.acceptance_test: After the revised equivalent of snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:183-203, every subsequent paragraph in the escape/ridge section visibly does one of four things: changes the escape situation, reveals a new survival fact, forces a new choice, or alters a relationship stance. If a later paragraph only rephrases Caul's broken faith or the System's betrayal without adding one of those concrete functions, the chapter still fails this test.
findings.problem: Caul's faith fracture lands when the creatures turn on his own party and his command voice breaks, but the chapter keeps re-proving the same realization through later diagnostic paraphrase. The back third adds explanation and confirmation more often than it adds new dramatic payload, which undercuts shortlist-level compression and leaves the chapter arriving at its point more than once.
findings.rewrite_direction: Compress or repurpose the explanatory coda across snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:183-203; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:229-239; snapshots/cycle_01/chapters/chapter_15.md:257-275. Keep the must-land beats: Caul abandons pursuit, Thessa gets Maren out, three sin-eaters are dead, and Maren leaves carrying that cost. After the initial break lands, later material should earn its space by adding a new fact, cost, irreversible choice, or relationship shift rather than restating that the System chose against its own heroes.

findings.acceptance_test: Ch 15 em-dash density is at or below 15% of prose lines.
findings.evidence: Ch 15 has 70 em-dash-containing prose lines out of 136 total prose lines = 51.5% density. Manuscript-wide: 979/2406 = 40.7%. Threshold: 10%.
findings.problem: Em-dash density in Ch 15 is 51.5%. The combat and reclassification sequence relies on em-dashes for nearly every tactical update, internal voice, and action beat, flattening the distinction between interruption and narration. When everything is interrupted, nothing is.
findings.rewrite_direction: Reduce em-dash-containing lines in Ch 15 from 70 to no more than 20 (targeting 15% density). Use periods for completed thoughts, commas for light asides, and reserve em-dashes for genuine mid-sentence breaks where the interruption carries meaning.

non_negotiables: Maintain heading contract exactly (# Chapter N).
Elliptical clipping is not permitted in dialogue or narration.
Avoid transcript cadence in high-stakes dialogue.
Avoid stiff over-formality in dialogue; use contractions and colloquial rhythm where character-true.
Preserve productive dialogue roughness; do not clean living speech into balanced thesis statements.
Reduce procedural drift when it displaces embodied consequence.
Maintain character-specific voice signatures from style bible.
Honor aesthetic risk policy: sanitization is the primary craft risk — do not soften, euphemize, or retreat from the story's established register. When in doubt, err toward rawness.

revision_pass.focus: Prioritize structural and craft blockers first: causality, chapter architecture, stakes clarity, continuity logic, and all HIGH/CRITICAL defects.
revision_pass.label: HIGH/CRITICAL structural/craft fixes

aesthetic_risk_policy.creative_risk_policy: This novel must commit fully to its own weirdness. A System-blind con man with a dead paladin and a dead assassin arguing in his skull is inherently absurd, and the novel should let that absurdity live alongside genuine grief, genuine violence, and genuine mystery without apologizing for the tonal range. The darkest moments and the funniest moments may occupy the same scene. When they do, let both registers operate simultaneously — do not sacrifice comedy for gravitas or gravitas for comedy. Risk means specificity: the specific sensation of a body doing things you did not tell it to do, the specific comedy of a dead paladin critiquing your hygiene while you're running for your life, the specific horror of carrying incomplete people whose memories have holes, the specific shame of learning that your eleven years of fraud may have killed people. When in doubt between too raw and too safe, choose raw. The novel's emotional range should be wider than any single genre label would suggest.
aesthetic_risk_policy.euphemism_penalty: Euphemism in the narration of violence, bodily experience, moral compromise, or physical consequence is a draft-level failure. Maren is a con man who has seen hard things and done dirty work in poor villages for eleven years — his narration should not flinch where he would not flinch. The style bible's sensory bias toward tactile and proprioceptive detail means physical consequence must be rendered through the body: what it feels like to throw up, to kill someone, to have your muscles operate without your consent, to perform a ritual that requires you to open yourself to contamination. Summary and abstraction at the moment of consequence is always worse than presence.

dialogue_rules.default_contraction_use: High — contractions are the norm for all characters except Aldric, whose low contraction level is a deliberate character marker that should erode over the course of the novel. Uncontracted forms in other characters' speech are reserved for emphasis, deliberation, or ceremony. In a world of practical people doing unglamorous work, stiff diction is the exception.

prose_style_profile.chapter_texture_variance: Alternate between hot and quiet chapters as a structural principle. No two consecutive chapters should open the same way — if one opens with action, the next should open with interiority or setting; if one opens with dialogue, the next should open with physical sensation or observation. Vary scene count between chapters: compressed single-scene chapters after multi-scene ones. Let quiet chapters stay quiet — do not insert action beats or revelations to maintain pace. The pacing engine is Maren's voice and the interplay between the three internal consciousnesses, not external plot pressure.
prose_style_profile.diction: Colloquial with earned elevation. Default register is smart but not literary — a con man's English, precise about people and vague about feelings. Elevation reserved for moments of genuine awe, grief, or the rare glimpse of something beautiful. Fantasy-register portentousness is a draft-level failure — no 'ancient darkness' or 'powers beyond comprehension.' The world is weird and dangerous and ordinary all at once, and the prose should match.
forbidden_drift_patterns: Sentence fragments imitating action-movie or thriller prose — subject-dropping and article-stripping for false urgency
Semicolon-heavy scholarly narration that distances the reader from embodied experience
Present-tense micro-shifts for intensity — the novel is past tense throughout, no exceptions
Filtering words ('he saw,' 'he felt,' 'he noticed,' 'he realized') when direct rendering is available
Fantasy-voice portentousness: 'ancient,' 'dark power,' 'something stirred,' 'a presence beyond reckoning'
Comic narration that winks at the reader or breaks the fourth wall instead of committing to the situation
Repeated metaphor templates — if a comparison structure appears twice in a chapter, the second instance is a tic
Emotional summary replacing scene presence: 'He was overwhelmed by grief' instead of rendering what grief looks and feels like in the body
prose_style_profile.narrative_distance: Close third person, locked to Maren throughout. Aldric and Vess are experienced as internal voices — they speak as italicized dialogue within Maren's consciousness, clearly distinguished from his own thoughts. Maren cannot access their thoughts directly; he hears what they say to him, not what they think. The narration inherits Maren's sensory limitations: the System interface is always mediated through Aldric or Vess's descriptions, never rendered directly until the single exception in Chapter 17.
prose_style_profile.rhythm_target: Varied by emotional register. Default: medium-length sentences with periodic short declaratives for grounding. When Maren is performing (lying, charming, faking): smoother, more controlled rhythm with longer subordinate-clause chains. When Maren is honest or overwhelmed: choppier, more fragmented, more concrete. Fight scenes: visceral and slightly disoriented, reflecting Maren's System-blindness — sensation and proprioception without the visual data everyone else has. Quiet scenes: allow longer, more observational sentences. The rhythm should breathe differently in every chapter.
sensory_bias: tactile — Maren's primary sense channel; the physical world is how he navigates System-blindness
auditory — the hum of System activity, Aldric and Vess's voices, the specific sounds of quest-light and contamination
proprioceptive — Maren's body doing things he didn't choose; the internal sensation of carrying fragments; the physical cost of consumption
olfactory — contamination has smell; ritual has smell; the world stinks in specific ways that the System-sighted don't notice because they navigate by interface
